hypotheses.name: Targeted intrusion (APT)
hypotheses.description: A sophisticated threat actor deliberately compromised this workstation as part of a targeted campaign against the shieldbase.lan enterprise network.
hypotheses.assessment: MOST PLAUSIBLE — zero inconsistencies, all evidence consistent with targeted APT lateral movement

hypotheses.name: Opportunistic malware
hypotheses.description: The system was infected by commodity malware via phishing or exploit kit.
hypotheses.assessment: ELIMINATED — WMI lateral movement (E-001), multiple staged execution chains (E-002), and SMB named pipe C2 (E-005) are inconsistent with automated commodity malware

hypotheses.name: Insider threat
hypotheses.description: An authorized user misused access to deploy tools on this workstation.
hypotheses.assessment: ELIMINATED — WMI remote execution from another host, shellcode injection, and named pipe C2 are inconsistent with insider activity patterns

hypotheses.name: Legitimate activity (null hypothesis)
hypotheses.description: All observed artifacts are from normal administration, software updates, or authorized maintenance.
hypotheses.assessment: ELIMINATED — encoded PowerShell stagers, reflective DLL injection, and named pipe C2 are not legitimate administration activities

hypotheses.name: Red team / penetration test
hypotheses.description: The observed Cobalt Strike activity is from an authorized security assessment.
hypotheses.assessment: POSSIBLE but less likely — TTPs are consistent with red team tooling (Cobalt Strike is common in pentests). However, the SRL scenario context (E-007) establishes this as a compromised network exercise, not a red team engagement. If this were a real investigation, H5 would require verification with the security team.

evidence_items.description: WMI lateral movement: WmiPrvSE.exe (PID 2676) spawned 3 PowerShell instances in Session 0, indicating remote execution from another host
Multiple staged execution chains: 64-bit PS → 32-bit PS (Wow64 cradle) → rundll32, repeated 3 times between 01:14-01:31 UTC on Aug 31
evidence_items.diagnostic_value: HIGH — methodical staging with Wow64 bypass indicates sophisticated operator, not automated malware

evidence_items.description: Encoded PowerShell stager: -nop -w hidden -encodedcommand with MemoryStream/gzip/base64 payload
evidence_items.diagnostic_value: MEDIUM — consistent with both APT and commodity malware using PowerShell Empire/Metasploit

evidence_items.description: Reflective DLL injection via VirtualAlloc(0x3000, 0x40) + GetDelegateForFunctionPointer shellcode execution
evidence_items.diagnostic_value: MEDIUM — common in both APT tools and frameworks, but inconsistent with legitimate activity

evidence_items.description: SMB Beacon C2 via named pipe \\.\ pipe\diagsvc-22 — Cobalt Strike signature
evidence_items.diagnostic_value: HIGHEST — SMB named pipe C2 is signature Cobalt Strike. Eliminates commodity malware and insider threat. Consistent with APT or red team.

evidence_items.description: Attacker evaded McAfee AV + Sysmon with Cobalt Strike detection rules — attack succeeded despite active defenses
Scenario context: SRL-2018 Compromised Enterprise Network exercise — this is known to be a compromised environment, not a red team exercise
evidence_items.diagnostic_value: HIGH for H5 discrimination — but this is external context, not forensic evidence

zero_diagnostic_items: None — all evidence items are inconsistent with at least one hypothesis

leading_hypothesis.name: Targeted intrusion (APT)
leading_hypothesis.rationale: Zero inconsistencies across 7 evidence items. All observed TTPs — WMI lateral movement, staged PowerShell Wow64 injection, Cobalt Strike SMB Beacon — are hallmarks of targeted APT operations. H5 (Red Team) is the only close competitor with 1 inconsistency, but scenario context eliminates it.

eliminated_hypotheses.name: Opportunistic malware
eliminated_hypotheses.reason: 3 inconsistencies: WMI lateral movement requires prior network access (not initial infection vector), staged Wow64 chains are operator-driven not automated, SMB named pipe C2 is not used by commodity malware families.

eliminated_hypotheses.name: Insider threat
eliminated_hypotheses.reason: 4 inconsistencies: WMI remote execution implies external origin, shellcode injection is not insider behavior, encoded commands suggest stealth (insiders use legitimate tools), named pipe C2 framework is attacker infrastructure.

eliminated_hypotheses.name: Legitimate activity
eliminated_hypotheses.reason: 6 inconsistencies: No legitimate administrative purpose for encoded PowerShell stagers, VirtualAlloc shellcode, Cobalt Strike named pipes, or rundll32 injection chains.

sensitivity_analysis.impact_if_wrong: If the named pipe is a legitimate service (e.g., diagnostic service), H1 loses its strongest Cobalt Strike indicator. The attack would still be confirmed by E-001/E-002/E-003/E-004, but attribution to Cobalt Strike specifically would weaken.
linchpin_assumptions: The named pipe diagsvc-22 is a C2 channel, not a legitimate Windows service pipe
The WMI execution originated from a remote host (not local task scheduler)
The shellcode in memory is the actual executed payload, not residual from a scan or test

confirm: Named pipe diagsvc-22 found on other workstations in the network → confirms lateral pivot chain
Cobalt Strike team server found at an internal or external IP
Additional memory dumps from other hosts show same Wow64 stager pattern
Sysmon logs on DC show WMI event correlating to Aug 31 01:14 UTC
Disk artifacts: PowerShell script block logs, Prefetch for powershell.exe timestamps
refute: Red team engagement documented for Aug 31 - Sep 6 2018 timeframe
diagsvc-22 is a legitimate named pipe from installed diagnostic software
WMI execution was scheduled by SCCM or other management tool (check __EventFilter in WMI repository)

findings.description: Targeted APT intrusion is the most plausible explanation. Zero inconsistencies across 7 evidence items. The combination of WMI lateral movement, staged Wow64 PowerShell injection, reflective DLL loading, and Cobalt Strike SMB Beacon C2 via named pipe diagsvc-22 is a textbook APT post-exploitation pattern. Commodity malware (H2) eliminated by the sophistication and operator-driven nature. Insider threat (H3) eliminated by remote execution indicators. Legitimate activity (H4) eliminated by 6 inconsistencies.
findings.citation: [INFERENCE: ACH analysis — H1 has 0 inconsistencies vs H2(3), H3(4), H4(6), H5(1). H5 eliminated by scenario context. All ratings traceable to volatility3.psscan and strings extraction findings. Confidence: HIGH]
